creator: imp@FreeBSD.org
text:
With repsect, pull requests are way easier to deal with. Everything is in git, rather than a hodge-podge of different systems to get the patch, get the commit message, get the author, discover that it's there in the first place. MFC is a hard problem for all contributions, but landing a pull request, and then MFCing it is all git and git cherry-pick -x works great. The larger problem of when contributed bugs should be MFC'd, though remains independent of means.

Please do not discourage pull requests.
